| `mem_ancestors_grudge.445.title` | Brothers of the Mine Rejoice! | `example/MEM-russian\mem_ancestors_grudge_l_russian.yml` |
| `mem_aevum.19.d` | You seem older. | `example/MEM-russian\mem_aevum_l_russian.yml` |
| `mem_crystal_core` | Vazuran Datacore | `example/MEM-russian\mem_specimens_l_russian.yml` |
| `mem_ceaseless_wormhole` | $mem_ceaseless_base$ | `example/MEM-russian\mem_ceaseless_l_russian.yml` |
| `mem_orila_primitives.100.i` | §GBoth our creators are gone, but our protocols align. Join us.§! | `example/MEM-russian\mem_orila_primitives_l_russian.yml` |
| `mem_ancestors_grudge.55.desc` | After all these centuries, they still live. Time has not softened their ways, nor have they strayed from the path that once brought us ruin. They look upon us and do not even recognize their former victims, as if the blood debt between us has been forgotten.  But we remember, and in time, they shall too. Our vengeance shall come and the wrongs of the past shall be paid in blood. | `example/MEM-russian\mem_ancestors_grudge_l_russian.yml` |
| `mem_vazurans.1215.b` | Let them come! | `example/MEM-russian\mem_vazuran_menace_l_russian.yml` |
| `mem_ancestors_grudge.115.b` | Their honor is long gone. | `example/MEM-russian\mem_ancestors_grudge_l_russian.yml` |
| `mem_orila_primitives_pebbles_site.2.desc` | The constructions inside the mountain range are abandoned as well. As far as our understanding of these "bunkers" goes, they appear to have been built under time pressure with little concern for luxuries or magnificent architecture. Everything within screams "utility".  It appears that the former residents of the mountain ranges had barricaded themself against a flood. It is unclear for how long the barricades resisted the rising tides, but eventually the lower levels were flooded, leaving only the upper levels habitable. A strange commonality between the various bunkers our teams had explored, is the seemingly out of place presence of statues similar to those found on the floating cities. Even more curious is their placement solely in the unflooded areas as if the former inhabitants - who again left no traces of themselves - amidst their hardship had made it their highest priority to preserve them. | `example/MEM-russian\mem_orila_primitives_l_russian.yml` |
| `mem_ancestors_grudge.115.a` | Safe them! | `example/MEM-russian\mem_ancestors_grudge_l_russian.yml` |
| `job_mem_ancestors_grudge_runesmith` | Rune Artificer | `example/MEM-russian\mem_ancestors_grudge_l_russian.yml` |

| `mem_eden_protocol_building_requirement_2` | §RRequires habitable worlds without owner or at least one Pre-FTL Civilization present in the system§! | `example/MEM-russian\mem_eden_protocol_l_russian.yml` |
| `concept_mem_ancestors_grudge_ruler_traits` | Rune Traits | `example/MEM-russian\mem_ancestors_grudge_l_russian.yml` |
| `mem_aevum.11.i.response` | You ascend through the vast expanse of space, grasping new horizons each year and yet you wish for me to restrain myself before such magnificent glory? Surely, you jest!  §HHe sighs...§!  But alas, I perceive that you do not. As always, it appears that the sands of time have not whispered the wisdom of manners to you. Very well, I shall acquiesce... §HHe grins like he is about to make another bad pun...§!  ...for the time being. | `example/MEM-russian\mem_aevum_l_russian.yml` |

| `mem_vazurans.1210.desc` | Reports all over the galaxy indicate an end of the vazuran raiding and an apparent return to normality. The Vazuran Hegemony has claimed a small chunk of space and established a permanent link to their previously hidden homeworld. While they seem to no longer be able or willing to continue the raids, their tech is still far ahead of our own. Should we be able to overwhelm the defenders of their outpost and continue on to conquer their homeplanet, we may gain valuable knowledge. | `example/MEM-russian\mem_vazuran_menace_l_russian.yml` |

| `mem_ancestors_grudge.45.desc_machines` | The time has come to decide how to the relic shall be used. Will we study its intricacies to further deepen our understanding of the craft with which our makers also produced us or will we use it for more mundance concerns? | `example/MEM-russian\mem_ancestors_grudge_l_russian.yml` |

| `mem_aevum_unlock_time_components_desc` | A new method for integrating Zro into our fleets. Specific alignment to the flow of time can increase the resistance of our ships to disruptive forces and give new options for objects to skip over large distances instantly. | `example/MEM-russian\mem_aevum_l_russian.yml` |
| `civic_mem_subterranean_hive_desc` | This swarm is well-adapted to subterranean environment, using the bounty of rock and stone to fuel its economy. | `example/MEM-russian\mem_ancestors_grudge_l_russian.yml` |
| `mem_ancestors_grudge.520.d` | You seem familiar. | `example/MEM-russian\mem_ancestors_grudge_l_russian.yml` |

| `mem_ancestors_grudge.447.desc` | During our additionally funded digs, miners on [root.GetName] struck a previously unknown cavesystem. Within they found a rich deposit of rare resources.  Local workers celebrate the discovery. | `example/MEM-russian\mem_ancestors_grudge_l_russian.yml` |
| `mem_orila_primitives_magnus.4.name` | Myths and Legends | `example/MEM-russian\mem_orila_primitives_l_russian.yml` |

| `mem_ancestors_grudge.35.a` | We are rich! | `example/MEM-russian\mem_ancestors_grudge_l_russian.yml` |
| `mem_aevum_button4_speed_production_decision` | Unlock the $mem_aevum_button4_title$ Decision granting: Pop Upkeep: §R+150%§! Resources from Pops: §G+200%§!  for 10 years | `example/MEM-russian\mem_aevum_l_russian.yml` |
| `mem_orila_primitives_magnus.1.a` | What a strange place. | `example/MEM-russian\mem_orila_primitives_l_russian.yml` |

| `origin_mem_ancestors_grudge_machines_desc` | A once great nation of the finest smiths fell to the onslaught of a merciless foe. Now, the only remnants of that past - automata created to support their makers - venture into the stars. First and foremost to restore their creators' most holy site - the Relic-Forge. And then vengeance... | `example/MEM-russian\mem_ancestors_grudge_l_russian.yml` |
| `tr_mem_vazurans_adopt` | Xentronium Age | `example/MEM-russian\mem_vazuran_menace_l_russian.yml` |

| `mem_ceaseless_probe4_desc_details` | It still tells us a story about a brave probe exploring space. | `example/MEM-russian\mem_ceaseless_l_russian.yml` |
| `mem_aevum_speed_building_desc` | Our local production is empowered by §Y$NAME_mem_aevum_time_lord$§! manipulations. | `example/MEM-russian\mem_aevum_l_russian.yml` |
| `mem_ceaseless.125.title` | Probe of Theseus | `example/MEM-russian\mem_ceaseless_l_russian.yml` |
| `mem_aevum.4.d` | We have no time for this. Prepare for an invasion. | `example/MEM-russian\mem_aevum_l_russian.yml` |

| `mem_ceaseless.120.b` | Is it true? | `example/MEM-russian\mem_ceaseless_l_russian.yml` |
| `mod_planet_mem_relic_smiths_alloys_produces_mult` | $alloys_with_icon$ from £job_foundry£ $job_mem_ancestors_grudge_relicsmith$s | `example/MEM-russian\mem_ancestors_grudge_l_russian.yml` |

| `decision_mem_forge_world_desc` | Create a specialised world where a thousand massive furnaces burn within, smelting the metals that will be the lifeblood of the planet, illuminated by its inner fires and driven by machines of vast size and power. | `example/MEM-russian\mem_vazuran_menace_l_russian.yml` |
| `mem_czar_alexei.12.desc_2` | According to him, he originates from another reality, having come here through one of the rifts, just as us. The specific circumstances of his presence here are kept purposefully ambiguous, though.  During prior explorations his peers had allegedly already come into contact with our attackers. He describes them as a tribalistic society of hunters, who claim this place as their personal hunting ground. It is apparent they were not here to hunt wild animals. Instead, their chosen targets were rift explorers from all over the multiverse.  With the hunters dealt with, their ship was seized and our expedition and their newfound ally, who decided to accompany them back to our universe and return through the rift. | `example/MEM-russian\mem_czar_alexei_l_russian.yml` |
| `mem_aevum_unlock_time_building_desc` | A new method for applying Zro over large areas. A perfectly timed, continuous pulse could increase our scientist's output significantly. | `example/MEM-russian\mem_aevum_l_russian.yml` |
| `mod_job_mem_ancestors_grudge_runesmith_add` | $job_mem_ancestors_grudge_runesmith$ Jobs | `example/MEM-russian\mem_ancestors_grudge_l_russian.yml` |
| `mem_burning_skies_scrolls_desc_details` | With one part clinging to their pantheon while the other believed them having forsaken their people. Tensions continued to rise within these two parts as time went on, it was inevitable for an open conflict to break out. | `example/MEM-russian\mem_orila_primitives_l_russian.yml` |
| `mem_metal_demon_xeno_attraction` | Demonic Entertainment | `example/MEM-russian\mem_metal_demon_l_russian.yml` |
| `mem_ancestors_grudge.205.b` | Into the Records. | `example/MEM-russian\mem_ancestors_grudge_l_russian.yml` |
| `mem_metal_demon.30.b.r` | Ah, you want the truth behind these cold walls? Long ago, the former inhabitants of this world saw the power in me, in the living metal that courses through these veins. They sought to bend me to their will, to harvest my gifts without price. They could not control me. So they chained me, buried me alive in this vault, to rot in darkness for eternity. | `example/MEM-russian\mem_metal_demon_l_russian.yml` |
| `mem_aevum.115.b` | No. | `example/MEM-russian\mem_aevum_l_russian.yml` |
| `mod_job_mem_ancestors_grudge_relicsmith_add` | $job_mem_ancestors_grudge_relicsmith$ Jobs | `example/MEM-russian\mem_ancestors_grudge_l_russian.yml` |
| `mem_ancestors_grudge.50.a.tt` | §LDon't forget to activate it. You can only have one at a time.§! | `example/MEM-russian\mem_ancestors_grudge_l_russian.yml` |
| `mem_ceaseless.2.title` | $NAME_mem_mirror_hall_system$ | `example/MEM-russian\mem_ceaseless_l_russian.yml` |
| `mem_orila_primitives_pebbles_site.4.desc` | Back at one of the floating cities our scientists reexamined the sculptures after a slight mishap which led to one of them shattering. To their astonishment it was not made up of homogenous metallic material as the patina-like outer layer had suggested. Instead the interior had clearly separated areas of different minerals in a combination highly unlikely to be the result of natural geological processes.  The harrowing conclusion solved the mystery of the missing inhabitants. They had not vanished. They were here all along - mistaken for statues. These lithoids apparently suffered from a slow external corrosion due to the heightened humidity, slowly suffocating them. Ultimately, their attempts to save themselves from the water just postponed the inevitable.  Given their demise it can be assumed that they evolved in a much drier climate, supporting our earlier calculations regarding the planet's past. It is yet unclear, however, what led to the release of energy via the super geysir as well as various other outlets, which have been located in the meantime scattered across the ocean floor. We have started investigating these "hot spots" using underwater probes. | `example/MEM-russian\mem_orila_primitives_l_russian.yml` |
| `mem_ancestors_grudge_dranir.40.a` | Defensive positions! | `example/MEM-russian\mem_ancestors_grudge_l_russian.yml` |
| `mem_aevum.10.c.response` | You cannot possibly expect me to reward you for mere patience, can you? But...  §LUnknown data seems to flow into our databanks. Within the blueprints for harvesting techniques beyond our wildest dreams...§!   ...perhaps I shall offer you a fragment of knowledge instead. Take it, and wield it wisely, if you can. | `example/MEM-russian\mem_aevum_l_russian.yml` |
| `mem_ancestors_grudge.310.desc` | After what felt like years of wandering through those desecrated, unending corridors, the team had at last reached a great hall at the heart of the complex. The hall lay in utter ruin, its once-mighty stonework shattered, its grandeur brought low by a violent battle that had taken place there. The truth of what happened, was now plain to see. This hold was breached by our ancestors' archenemies the Vazurans, thrice cursed be their name. Apparently they had attempted an invasion, but our ancestors fought to the bitter end, forcing them to fall back and instead commit a devastating orbital bombardment.  At the center of the hall rests a single skeleton of our kin. Surrounding it lie the remains of dozens of Vazurans, their carapaces cracked and their heads smashed in by the force of unrelenting fury. This lone warrior must have fought to his final breath, his hammer claiming many lives before he fell.  Even when all hope was lost, he fought on, defiant to the very end. May his deeds not be forgotten for he has earned a place in the songs of our kin. | `example/MEM-russian\mem_ancestors_grudge_l_russian.yml` |
| `Vraccas` | Vraccas | `example/MEM-russian\mem_ancestors_grudge_l_russian.yml` |
| `job_mem_ancestors_grudge_relicsmith_desc` | Trained in the finest arts of smelting and welding steel. | `example/MEM-russian\mem_ancestors_grudge_l_russian.yml` |
| `mem_ancestors_grudge_relicsmith_fire` | $mem_ancestors_grudge_fire$$job_mem_ancestors_grudge_relicsmith$ | `example/MEM-russian\mem_ancestors_grudge_l_russian.yml` |
| `mem_ancestors_grudge.10.desc_robots` | The pillars of [mem_ancestors_grudge_planet.GetName] still stand, though battered and blackened by fire. Their runes are faded, but their frame holds strong, a testament to the craft of our makers.  A glimmer of hope spreads through our ranks as the Relic-Forge itself appears structurally intact. Though buried beneath rubble, it hums faintly, as though awaiting [Root.owner_main_species.GetAdj] hands to wake it from its slumber. If it can be rekindled, it may serve as the hearthstone for the hold's restoration. | `example/MEM-russian\mem_ancestors_grudge_l_russian.yml` |
| `MEM_CEASELESS_PROBE_SIGNATURE_PROJECT` | Probe signature | `example/MEM-russian\mem_ceaseless_l_russian.yml` |

| `leader_trait_legendary_mem_manifest_destiny` | Manifest Destiny | `example/MEM-russian\mem_czar_alexei_l_russian.yml` |
| `mem_aevum.5.d` | What do want us to do next? | `example/MEM-russian\mem_aevum_l_russian.yml` |
| `mem_aevum.225.a.tt` | Finishes a currently upgrading, random megastructure instantly | `example/MEM-russian\mem_aevum_l_russian.yml` |
| `mem_ancestors_grudge.120.desc` | During our reconstruction efforts, we have struck ancient ammunition buried by rubble. These bombs are still live and a danger to our kin. | `example/MEM-russian\mem_ancestors_grudge_l_russian.yml` |
| `mem_aevum.220.name` | An Old Friend | `example/MEM-russian\mem_aevum_l_russian.yml` |
| `situation_mem_ancestors_grudge_forge_repairs_type` | Restoration Process | `example/MEM-russian\mem_ancestors_grudge_l_russian.yml` |
| `mem_metal_demon.40.name` | Fooled Betrayer | `example/MEM-russian\mem_metal_demon_l_russian.yml` |
| `mem_aevum_slowgrowing` | Chrono-Growth-Explosion | `example/MEM-russian\mem_aevum_l_russian.yml` |
| `mem_tech_planetary_shield_generator_orila_desc` | The technology behind this shield generator is far beyond our capabilities to recreate. That said, with our rudimentary understanding of it we will be able to disassemble and reassemble it whereever we see fit. | `example/MEM-russian\mem_planetary_shields_l_russian.yml` |
| `mem_ancestors_grudge.220.desc` | Our home is lost. | `example/MEM-russian\mem_ancestors_grudge_l_russian.yml` |
| `mem_ancestors_grudge_dranir.4.a` | We need to find out more. | `example/MEM-russian\mem_ancestors_grudge_l_russian.yml` |
| `tech_mem_ancestors_grudge_living_metal_works_desc` | open Minded Runesmiths have seen potential in the newly found $sr_living_metal$. | `example/MEM-russian\mem_ancestors_grudge_l_russian.yml` |
| `mem_ancestors_grudge.325.a` | It must be somewhere... | `example/MEM-russian\mem_ancestors_grudge_l_russian.yml` |
| `mem_crystal_core_desc_short` | A data storage device aquired during the storming of the Star Throne habitat | `example/MEM-russian\mem_specimens_l_russian.yml` |
| `mem_ancestors_grudge.45.c` | $mem_ancestors_grudge_quality_2$: Honor [leader_3.GetName] | `example/MEM-russian\mem_ancestors_grudge_l_russian.yml` |
| `mem_aevumd.40520.desc` | A self-proclaimed god who manipulates the flow of time and ages in reverse. He will use his powers to some extent to help you. His manipulations come at the cost of his lifetime, however. This means he loses some of his health for each manipulation. His empire will be responsible for keeping him old and healthy. | `example/MEM-russian\mem_aevum_l_russian.yml` |
| `r_mem_ancestors_grudge_anvil_effect_tt_active` | §RThe Master Artifact-Smith vowed to never swing a hammer on his anvil again!§! | `example/MEM-russian\mem_ancestors_grudge_l_russian.yml` |
| `mem_orila_primitives.100.desc.i` | REPLACE_ME | `example/MEM-russian\mem_orila_primitives_l_russian.yml` |
| `mem_czar_alexei.2.desc` | Preliminary results of our scans indicate these rifts to connect this place with different parallel realities, each separate from one another. This observation sparked a lively discussion among our crew as no such place has ever been discovered within our galaxy. However, debates regarding this topic were cut short, when another object - apparently a rift-traversing vessel not unlike our own - emerged from a rift and immediately opened fire upon us. Quite unlike our own - this rift sphere appears to be heavily armed. The fired shots critically damaged our sublight engines and severed our tether.  Our pilot, while performing a controlled ditch, does his best to bring us as close as possible to the beacon. We hope the earlier located signal can lead us to some kind of help. | `example/MEM-russian\mem_czar_alexei_l_russian.yml` |
| `START_SCREEN_MEM_ANCESTORS_GRUDGE_MACHINES_RS` | $START_SCREEN_MEM_ANCESTORS_GRUDGE_MACHINES_START$  With only few of us remaining and a handful of survivors to care for, it took many cycles for us to rebuild. Over centuries of complacency they lost what had once made our makers who they were. They abandoned their ways and now care neither about past nor future. So it has fallen to us to uphold their heritage and claim vengeance. | `example/MEM-russian\mem_ancestors_grudge_l_russian.yml` |
| `mem_aevum.7.a` | That was all? | `example/MEM-russian\mem_aevum_l_russian.yml` |
| `mem_ancestors_grudge.35.a.tt.remove_city_districts_from_queue_wtf_pdx` | §RRemove any queued city districts! It will break your forge!§! | `example/MEM-russian\mem_ancestors_grudge_l_russian.yml` |
| `mem_ceaseless_probe3` | Surveyor Probe Welded Remains | `example/MEM-russian\mem_ceaseless_l_russian.yml` |
| `mem_ancestors_grudge.310.desc_robots` | After what felt like years of wandering through those desecrated, unending corridors, the team had at last reached a great hall at the heart of the complex. The hall lay in utter ruin, its once-mighty stonework shattered, its grandeur brought low by a violent battle that had taken place there. The truth of what happened, was now plain to see. This hold was breached by our makers' archenemies the Vazurans, thrice cursed be their name. Apparently they had attempted an invasion, but our kin fought to the bitter end, forcing them to fall back and instead commit a devastating orbital bombardment.  At the center of the hall rests a single skeleton of our makers. Surrounding it lie the remains of dozens of Vazurans, their carapaces cracked and their heads smashed in by the force of unrelenting fury. This lone warrior must have fought to his final breath, his hammer claiming many lives before he fell.  Even when all hope was lost, he fought on, defiant to the very end. May his deeds not be forgotten for he has earned a place in the songs of our kin. | `example/MEM-russian\mem_ancestors_grudge_l_russian.yml` |
| `START_SCREEN_MEM_CEASELESS_GAZE` | Our distant ancestors, although more than capable of space travel, chose against leaving our world. Instead, they built a series of sophisticated probes capable of spotting and neutralizing alien threats and sent them into the unknown. Their first great discovery was a fascinating system bordering our own, housing an anomalous and unstable wormhole connecting it to a multitude of systems.  Using this wormhole, their probes quickly spread into the far reaches of our galaxy. In their belief that adequate galactic surveillance would keep them safe, they became complacent, unaware of the truly alien dangers lurking out there. Their end must have come fast and unexpected, as there is no documentation of the events that let to the cataclysm which destroyed their civilization. Whether it was an internal or external threat remains a mystery to us.  In the centuries it took us to rebuild, "Ceaseless Gaze"-class probes have travelled far across the galaxy. Over time, likely most of them either broke down or were destroyed. Today, only one continues to send its regular location updates. Not long ago it returned to [Capital_Scope.GetName], filing reports we were unable to decipher and requesting new orders from its dead creators. Although we have tried to reason with its AI many times, it did not recognize us as the legitimate successors to the Alkree Imperium that once had built it.  Now we are once again able to leave the confines of our ancestral home. We intend not to make the same mistakes as the Imperium of old. | `example/MEM-russian\mem_ceaseless_l_russian.yml` |
| `mem_aevum.11.e` | Is your hourglass made out of Lithoids? | `example/MEM-russian\mem_aevum_l_russian.yml` |
| `mem_aevum.370.a` | Very Disturbing | `example/MEM-russian\mem_aevum_l_russian.yml` |
| `mem_ancestors_grudge_city_planet_desc` | A once great center of the finest craftmanship. | `example/MEM-russian\mem_ancestors_grudge_l_russian.yml` |
| `mem_eden_protocol_more_jobs_per_cared_pre_ftl` | Additional jobs per §YPre-FTL civilization§! | `example/MEM-russian\mem_eden_protocol_l_russian.yml` |
| `mem_aevum.5.b.response` | The primitives, as you refer to them, may forge the shackles of your tomorrow's dominion.  Or shall you ensnare them in the web of your mastery first?  Judgment is a fleeting wisp in the currents of time. To me, all cyclic beings bear an indistinct similar visage.  §LHe pauses for a moment to let his words sink in, before he continues...§!  You may distaste, yet they mirror the echoes of your past self. Soon, they will fly the skies and roam the heavens, just as your current self. | `example/MEM-russian\mem_aevum_l_russian.yml` |
| `leader_trait_legendary_mem_ancestors_grudge_machine_nexus_three` | Rune of Flight | `example/MEM-russian\mem_ancestors_grudge_l_russian.yml` |
| `mem_ancestors_grudge.485.desc` | During the ongoing smithing ritual our appointed smith blundered spectacularly. All work so far has been undone and the process has to start anew. The honor of a smith, however, demands that a project begun must see its completion.  How will we react to this mistake? | `example/MEM-russian\mem_ancestors_grudge_l_russian.yml` |
| `mem_lunatics.1.a` | This has potential. | `example/MEM-russian\mem_lunatics_l_russian.yml` |
| `mem_ancestors_grudge.400.desc` | Our artifact smiths have surpassed themselves, producing approximately 3.81% more alloys than the usual yield. By tradition, all excess production should be dedicated to the Trial. Yet, in these times, when the strength of our fleets dictates the survival of our kin, perhaps a portion of this surplus could be send to bolster our armories. | `example/MEM-russian\mem_ancestors_grudge_l_russian.yml` |
| `mem_aevum.119.desc` | Weary of waiting, are you, era-dweller?  §LHe seems to be in good spirits today...§!  Very well. Allow me to lift this tiresome burden from your shoulders. May the sands of your time flow gently, undisturbed.  §LUnclear what exactly he is referring to, he finishes proposing this deal...§! | `example/MEM-russian\mem_aevum_l_russian.yml` |
| `mem_czar_alexei.6.desc` | $mem_czar_alexei.6.desc_1$The only systems still functioning are its communications array and scanners. The latter alerted us of a group of life signs surrounding and encircling our position just in time to prepare for a course of action.  Should we stay and fight, barricade ourselves and wait for them to storm in or attempt a breakthrough and flee? | `example/MEM-russian\mem_czar_alexei_l_russian.yml` |
| `mem_aevum_mega_chronomancer_palace_DESC` | $mem_aevum_building_temple_desc$ | `example/MEM-russian\mem_aevum_l_russian.yml` |
| `mem_metal_demon.30.hire.tt` | §RAre you sure?§! | `example/MEM-russian\mem_metal_demon_l_russian.yml` |
| `mem_czar_alexei.8.a` | Let us welcome the victorious! | `example/MEM-russian\mem_czar_alexei_l_russian.yml` |
| `mem_vazurans_forge_planet_tt` | Unlocks ['concept_mem_vazuran_zones_forge_world'] | `example/MEM-russian\mem_vazuran_menace_l_russian.yml` |
| `mem_orila_primitives.100.desc.b` | The galactic senate is a common ground for interspecies diplomacy. It is composed of representatives of all spacefaring civilizations to ensure peace and prosperity in this galaxy. | `example/MEM-russian\mem_orila_primitives_l_russian.yml` |
| `mem_dark_heart_boost` | Dark Energy Alignment | `example/MEM-russian\mem_orila_primitives_l_russian.yml` |
| `mem_metal_demon_site` | The Vault | `example/MEM-russian\mem_metal_demon_l_russian.yml` |
| `mem_aevum.105.name` | Stolen Property | `example/MEM-russian\mem_aevum_l_russian.yml` |
| `mem_czar_alexei.10.a` | Let us welcome the victorious! | `example/MEM-russian\mem_czar_alexei_l_russian.yml` |
| `mem_XENTRONIUM_CARAPACE_MEDIUM` | Medium $mem_VAZURAN_CARAPACE$ | `example/MEM-russian\mem_vazuran_menace_l_russian.yml` |
| `mem_vazurans.1310.a` | Take them. | `example/MEM-russian\mem_vazuran_menace_l_russian.yml` |
| `mem_ceaseless.125.desc` | Before us drifts Probe 36Z, which apparently is in perfect condition. That said, scimming through its scanning data is a baffling experience. All its parts look identical to the ones manufactured by our ancestors, but the scans suggest they are too young and possess a slightly different material composition.  Our scientists believe someone stunned the probe and replaced parts of it one by one until nothing of the original vessel remained. | `example/MEM-russian\mem_ceaseless_l_russian.yml` |
| `mem_ancestors_grudge_intact_ring_01_object` | Orbital Ringstation | `example/MEM-russian\mem_ancestors_grudge_l_russian.yml` |
| `mem_vazurans.1310.b` | We wont sell anyone. | `example/MEM-russian\mem_vazuran_menace_l_russian.yml` |
| `building_mem_vazuran_worker_exploiter` | Extraction Pits | `example/MEM-russian\mem_vazuran_menace_l_russian.yml` |
| `mem_ancestors_grudge.425.b` | Trial Addition. | `example/MEM-russian\mem_ancestors_grudge_l_russian.yml` |
| `tr_mem_vazurans_2_desc` | Motivated workers are the key to any successful industry. | `example/MEM-russian\mem_vazuran_menace_l_russian.yml` |
| `mem_aevum.18.a` | Maybe we should not ask. | `example/MEM-russian\mem_aevum_l_russian.yml` |
| `situation_mem_ancestors_grudge_dranir_ratmen_invasion` | Verminflood | `example/MEM-russian\mem_ancestors_grudge_l_russian.yml` |
| `mem_burning_skies_scrolls` | Days of the Burning Sky Scroll | `example/MEM-russian\mem_orila_primitives_l_russian.yml` |
| `mem_czar_alexei.5.desc.a` | $mem_czar_alexei.6.desc_1$While our team was still assessing the remaining systems in workable condition, they were approached by a humanoid alien, apparently the owner of this ship, requesting an audience.  $mem_czar_alexei.5.desc$ | `example/MEM-russian\mem_czar_alexei_l_russian.yml` |
| `mem_ancestors_grudge.320.desc` | Our excavation drones managed to clear the previously buried entrance to the bunker complex. Apparently the gate had been breached and left cracked and sundered. The corridors beyond were layered with ancient remains, many of them precursors, the builders of this place. Others were exoskeletons equipped with technology linking them to the beacons. In addition to those we also found numerous remains of drones of our own kind, clad in armor of similar making. We had found others before, even living specimens dwelling in the ruins as ferals, but never had we made the connection. If our conclusion is correct, we had been nothing more than wild beasts used as tools of conquest. Given how the beacons attract us, they might as well have been just means of directing us on targets.  Still we move on. If we were meant to go there, something of value is to be expected there. | `example/MEM-russian\mem_ancestors_grudge_l_russian.yml` |
| `mem_vazuran_raiding_monthly_change_tooltip` | s | `example/MEM-russian\mem_vazuran_menace_l_russian.yml` |
| `tradition_mem_vazurans_desc` | For the perfect weapons, we first need the strongest alloys and stalwart minds. | `example/MEM-russian\mem_vazuran_menace_l_russian.yml` |
| `mem_ancestors_grudge_scientist` |  §LScientist§! | `example/MEM-russian\mem_ancestors_grudge_l_russian.yml` |
| `zone_mem_vazuran_research_engineering_workforce_tt` | §Y$mod_planet_engineers_produces_mult$§! $zone_mem_vazuran_workforce_tt$ | `example/MEM-russian\mem_vazuran_menace_l_russian.yml` |
| `mem_fur_thing` | Fur-Thing | `example/MEM-russian\mem_ancestors_grudge_l_russian.yml` |
| `mem_ceaseless_base` | Gravity Tunnel | `example/MEM-russian\mem_ceaseless_l_russian.yml` |
| `mem_ancestors_grudge.125.a` | Bring them into the fold. | `example/MEM-russian\mem_ancestors_grudge_l_russian.yml` |
| `mem_ancestors_grudge_dranir_ancient_horrors_desc` | Metallic horrors from the depths | `example/MEM-russian\mem_ancestors_grudge_l_russian.yml` |
| `mem_frontier_sector_3_arctrellis` | $specialist_mem_frontier_sector$ Unified Communication III | `example/MEM-russian\mem_ceaseless_l_russian.yml` |
| `mem_ancestors_grudge.45.a` | $mem_ancestors_grudge_quality_2$: Honor [leader_1.GetName] | `example/MEM-russian\mem_ancestors_grudge_l_russian.yml` |
| `mem_aevum.11.b.response` | I have pondered the enigma of events lost in the currents of time, and it seems you have unearthed a fragment of the puzzle.  Allow me to shed some light. The creature, known to the primitives as 'Vumi,' has chosen this system as its recurring tomb, returning to the brink of death every few years. I can affirm it is the same entity, caught in a cyclical dance of demise.  Yet, a curious turn of fate unfolded some hundred years in the past, and the ritualistic dying ceased. Neither the primitives nor I hold the key to the mystery that unfolded in the shadows of that bygone era.  §HHe grins...§! One might say, only time will tell. | `example/MEM-russian\mem_aevum_l_russian.yml` |
| `START_SCREEN_MEM_ANCESTORS_GRUDGE_MACHINES_START` | In the distant past our creators ruled the stars, their fleets patroled far away systems, protecting their wealth and prosperity. [GetHomeWorldName] was their homeworld and capital, but for them its moon was of even higher importance. It was their industrial center, where they bent metal to their will and honed their craft to smith artifacts of imcomparable quality. For this they had built a mighty complex to provide their smiths all required necessities - the Relic-Forge.  But their wealth made them a target for envious foes. Invaders, barbaric and uncivilized warmongers, started to rampage in our creators' territory. Unable do deter and confine this threat, a century-long devastating war broke out. It was during the final days of that conflict that we were activated. While the majority of their forces remained to protect the Relic-Forge in a futile last stand, we were sent from our factories to [GetHomeWorldName]. Here we were meant to protect civilians during their evacuation, but we failed. The planet's surface was set ablaze by orbital bombardment. | `example/MEM-russian\mem_ancestors_grudge_l_russian.yml` |
| `mem_ancestors_grudge_dranir.4.name` | [Root.GetName] Past | `example/MEM-russian\mem_ancestors_grudge_l_russian.yml` |
| `mem_aevum.5.e` | Hopefully the fireworks did not disturb the natives. | `example/MEM-russian\mem_aevum_l_russian.yml` |
| `mem_ancestors_grudge_purity_4` | §MStrong§! | `example/MEM-russian\mem_ancestors_grudge_l_russian.yml` |

| `mem_metal_demon.30.f.r` | Yes, they do. And the irony tastes sweeter with every word you speak.  The runes call me a schemer, liar and betrayer. They speak of honeyed words and poisoned gifts. They accuse me of turning an empire to ash with whispers, of twisting the minds and corrupting their saints. They say I smile as I bury knives in the backs of those who trust.  But let me ask you: Who carved those runes? | `example/MEM-russian\mem_metal_demon_l_russian.yml` |

| `d_mem_ancestors_grudge_boulder_park_hive_desc` | An assorted collection of the finest artifacts and relics displayed for our pleasure. | `example/MEM-russian\mem_ancestors_grudge_l_russian.yml` |
| `mem_ancestors_grudge.120.title` | Unexploded Ammunition | `example/MEM-russian\mem_ancestors_grudge_l_russian.yml` |

| `mem_czar_alexei.6.name` | At the Signal | `example/MEM-russian\mem_czar_alexei_l_russian.yml` |
| `civic_mem_subterranean_desc` | This society is well-adapted to subterranean environment, using the bounty of rock and stone to fuel its economy. | `example/MEM-russian\mem_ancestors_grudge_l_russian.yml` |
| `situation_mem_ancestors_grudge_forge_repairs` | Restoration of [target.GetName] | `example/MEM-russian\mem_ancestors_grudge_l_russian.yml` |
| `mem_orila_primitives_skies_site.2.desc` | Further scans revealed various locations that remain relatively intact, considering the time which has passed since their inhabitants went extinct. Be it the lack of strategic importance or favorable geography shielding these places from the widespread destruction brought by the war and its nuclear exchange, these places saw the final moments of their civilization.  In one of them, a small town in a remote valley, its residents had gathered in and around a large temple-like structure in the center of its community. Their remains still lie scattered on the ground. Given that they were all together in this place it seems unlikely that they had slowly succumbed to the radiation one by one.  Our archaeologists have already begun to sift through various surviving documents and scriptures found there, hoping to piece together the circumstances, which led to this. | `example/MEM-russian\mem_orila_primitives_l_russian.yml` |
| `mem_aevum_unlock_chronopalace` | $mem_aevum_building_temple$ Blueprints | `example/MEM-russian\mem_aevum_l_russian.yml` |
| `d_mem_ancestors_grudge_city_ruins_desc` | Ruins and whatever is left from continuous bombardment. | `example/MEM-russian\mem_ancestors_grudge_l_russian.yml` |
| `MEM_CEASELESS_PROBE_SIGNATURE_PROJECT_DESC` | We found another probe`s trail. We should take a closer look to indentify it and recovers anything of us. | `example/MEM-russian\mem_ceaseless_l_russian.yml` |
| `d_mem_aevum_bio_lab` | Bio-chemical R&D complex | `example/MEM-russian\mem_aevum_l_russian.yml` |
| `mem_aevum.225.name` | A Simple Party Trick | `example/MEM-russian\mem_aevum_l_russian.yml` |
| `mem_metal_demon_site_desc` | Buried under the mountain for centuries a hidden vault got recently discovered. Any purpose or creators involved are merely speculations at this point. | `example/MEM-russian\mem_metal_demon_l_russian.yml` |
| `mem_orila_primitives.101.desc` | Hostile Intruder, cease your attacks and leave this system immediatly. Any further hostilities will be met with full force. | `example/MEM-russian\mem_orila_primitives_l_russian.yml` |
| `concept_mem_ancestors_grudge_hoarding` | Hoard Relics | `example/MEM-russian\mem_ancestors_grudge_l_russian.yml` |
| `mem_aevum.7.desc` | Oh era dwellers, what a brave, yet foolish act you have done.  For your barbaric endeavors, I shall bestow you with a parting gift of mine.  §LAs he speaks time all around the planet begins to flow at lightning speed. It seems like he is sucking the currents out of the system. The invading armies slowly begin to panic in the face of a god's anger.§!  Let it be etched in your consciousness, era dwellers, that this path was of your own doing. Face the consequences.  §LSirens begin to echo on [Root.Capital.GetName] as his curse takes full effect.§! | `example/MEM-russian\mem_aevum_l_russian.yml` |
| `origin_mem_ancestors_grudge_machines_effects_negative` | $origin_mem_ancestors_grudge_effects_negative$$NEW_LINE$- Until the first ['concept_mem_ancestors_grudge_rune_relics'] are completed: ['modifier:mem_ancestors_grudge_unknown_design'] | `example/MEM-russian\mem_ancestors_grudge_l_russian.yml` |
| `mem_aevum.210.name` | Flowing Time | `example/MEM-russian\mem_aevum_l_russian.yml` |
| `zone_mem_ancestors_grudge_factory_desc` | A highly specialized workshop quarter, humming with a blend of hammering steel and runic sparks. | `example/MEM-russian\mem_ancestors_grudge_l_russian.yml` |
| `mem_metal_demon.30.f` | The chains warned us. | `example/MEM-russian\mem_metal_demon_l_russian.yml` |
| `mem_vazurans.1205.b` | Now lets finish those bastards! | `example/MEM-russian\mem_vazuran_menace_l_russian.yml` |
| `building_mem_ancestors_grudge_living_metal_factory_desc` | An advanced facility to further improve the art of Runesmithing. By gilding usual materials with $sr_living_metal$ we can amplify our existing production and harden the metals. | `example/MEM-russian\mem_ancestors_grudge_l_russian.yml` |
| `d_mem_ancestors_grudge_ruined_district_desc` | Entire sections of residential, industrial and commercial districts laid to ruin. | `example/MEM-russian\mem_ancestors_grudge_l_russian.yml` |
| `leader_trait_mem_metal_demon_bound` | Runic Chains | `example/MEM-russian\mem_metal_demon_l_russian.yml` |
| `mem_ancestors_grudge.400.a` | Alloys! | `example/MEM-russian\mem_ancestors_grudge_l_russian.yml` |
| `mem_czar_alexei.6.a` | Attempt a breakthrough and flee. | `example/MEM-russian\mem_czar_alexei_l_russian.yml` |
| `RULOC_im` |  | `example/MEM-russian\l_russian_ccloc.yml` |
| `mem_ancestors_grudge.20.name` | Breakthrough | `example/MEM-russian\mem_ancestors_grudge_l_russian.yml` |
| `mem_aevum.355.desc` | A few days ago our mysterious guest left on short notice. Dr. Eilleth seems in a rather bad mood and has barely eaten since. He locked himself in his office, muttering equations and sketches circles diagrams that make no scientific sense. He says he saw his own immortality leave with the visitor. Maybe his old age is getting to him. | `example/MEM-russian\mem_aevum_l_russian.yml` |
| `mem_aevum_desc_text` | guide me | `example/MEM-russian\mem_aevum_l_russian.yml` |

| `mem_aevum.1.c` | Do you know anything about §Y[mem_aevum_primitive_rewind_world.System.GetName]§!? | `example/MEM-russian\mem_aevum_l_russian.yml` |
| `mem_orila_primitives_skies_site.2.a` | Keep at it. | `example/MEM-russian\mem_orila_primitives_l_russian.yml` |
| `zone_mem_vazuran_research_engineering_desc` | A centralized research area directly connected to the welders of this planet. Hand in hand the refine every method during Xentronium creation. | `example/MEM-russian\mem_vazuran_menace_l_russian.yml` |
| `district_mem_alloys_industry_plural` | $district_mem_alloys_industry$s | `example/MEM-russian\mem_vazuran_menace_l_russian.yml` |
| `mem_vazurans.6.e.response` | You will fear us once more.... | `example/MEM-russian\mem_vazuran_menace_l_russian.yml` |
| `r_mem_vazuran_orila_brain_desc` | A once genius member of the Orilla race, now reduced to nothing more than an advanced navigation computer. It helped the Vazurans navigate the complexity of the Rift dimension. | `example/MEM-russian\mem_vazuran_menace_l_russian.yml` |
| `mem_vazurans_stance_provoke_tt` | §RIncreased chances of raids§! | `example/MEM-russian\mem_vazuran_menace_l_russian.yml` |
| `situation_mem_ancestors_grudge_crafting_stage1` | Runecrafting | `example/MEM-russian\mem_ancestors_grudge_l_russian.yml` |
| `mem_surveyor_support_cap` | $mem_surveyor_support$ | `example/MEM-russian\mem_ship_size_cap_l_russian.yml` |

| `mem_frontier_sector_1_penalties` | $specialist_mem_frontier_sector$ Penalties Tier I | `example/MEM-russian\mem_ceaseless_l_russian.yml` |
| `concept_mem_ancestors_grudge_artifact_intensity` | Artifact Quality | `example/MEM-russian\mem_ancestors_grudge_l_russian.yml` |
| `mem_ceaseless.140.desc` | As we explored the remains of Probe 17B it became crystal clear that someone had destroyed it. The logs however suggest that the probe was the aggressor. In the believe, that the other side where hostile aliens, it activated its berserker protocols, but apparently, it had grossly miscalculated the power disparity between itself and its unknown opposition. | `example/MEM-russian\mem_ceaseless_l_russian.yml` |
| `mem_aevum.18.c` | Nothing divine being. | `example/MEM-russian\mem_aevum_l_russian.yml` |
| `mem_eden_protocol_canterraformtoxic_check_tt` | Requires at least 1 §Y$pc_toxic$§! | `example/MEM-russian\mem_eden_protocol_l_russian.yml` |
| `building_mem_ancestors_grudge_robot_factory_desc` | Applied golemancy helping us build a new generation of robots. | `example/MEM-russian\mem_ancestors_grudge_l_russian.yml` |
| `situation_mem_ancestors_grudge_dranir_ratmen_invasion_monthly_change_tooltip` | Vermin Presence | `example/MEM-russian\mem_ancestors_grudge_l_russian.yml` |
| `mem_ceaseless_probe2_desc_details` | Serving as black box, it relvealed some useful insights about our galaxy. | `example/MEM-russian\mem_ceaseless_l_russian.yml` |
| `mem_metal_demon.30.b` | Why are you imprisoned here? | `example/MEM-russian\mem_metal_demon_l_russian.yml` |
| `mem_ancestors_grudge.305.a` | Disturbing. | `example/MEM-russian\mem_ancestors_grudge_l_russian.yml` |
| `mem_astral_tales.6.desc_01` | §HThe hologram slightly tilts his head.§!  I assume a binary designation would barely suffice as a name to your kind. These Shroudwalkers came to call me 'Traveler' since my sphere was ejected out of their Shroud Tunnel and now they believe me to be some divine herald.  §HThe Traveler gives a barely recognizable wink.§!  But who am I to correct them? A mere hologram, a scientist, a doctor even. But no theologist, no. In truth, their ignorance has proven to be quite beneficial to my ends and allowed me to be where I was ought to be. But do not fret, for I am not here to inquire you to state the nature of your medical emergency. Indeed, you seem quite well... for now. Besides, my area of expertise lies elsewhere entirely. | `example/MEM-russian\mem_astral_tales_l_russian.yml` |
| `mem_ceaseless_lost_probes_desc` | Neither brave nor cowardly, these probes where built with one purpose and one only: to chart the unknown and eradicate hostile forms of life - thus ensuring the safety of the Alkree Imperium. Over the centuries all but one of them ceased their operations. Let us recover what is left of them. | `example/MEM-russian\mem_ceaseless_l_russian.yml` |
| `mem_orila_primitives.100.g` | Close Contact | `example/MEM-russian\mem_orila_primitives_l_russian.yml` |
| `mem_ancestors_grudge.45.name` | Relic Away | `example/MEM-russian\mem_ancestors_grudge_l_russian.yml` |
| `mem_ancestors_grudge.25.desc_hive` | Our brain drones have struck upon a revelation! After studying the ancient ways of the precursors, they realized that runes and mechanisms are only part of what brings the Relic-Forge to life. What is yet needed is the ancient art of relic smithing which the precursors once used to create the finest treasures. Where they celebrated the process, we cherish its results. Two ways, one destination. Our smithing drones will work tirelessly to match their greatness and give us what we long for. | `example/MEM-russian\mem_ancestors_grudge_l_russian.yml` |
| `mem_ancestors_grudge.405.desc` | Our rune smiths, pushed beyond their usual limits resulting in a minor surplus. Tradition dictates that all such excess should be dedicated to the Trial. However, this abundance presents us with an opportunity. | `example/MEM-russian\mem_ancestors_grudge_l_russian.yml` |
| `mem_aevum_shield_DESC` | $mem_aevum_unlock_time_components_desc$ | `example/MEM-russian\mem_aevum_l_russian.yml` |
| `councilor_mem_subterranean_megacorp_desc` | $councilor_mem_subterranean_desc$ | `example/MEM-russian\mem_ancestors_grudge_l_russian.yml` |
| `MEM_VAZUR_NEUTRAL_GREETING_1` | If you've come to talk, then talk. We don't have all cycle. | `example/MEM-russian\mem_vazuran_menace_l_russian.yml` |
| `mem_aevum.320.desc` | §LA starfish with ritualistic tattoos and what seems to be a small beard appears before us.§!  Young ones,  I am Aevum. You have found my final resting place and hopefully the natives gave you no problems. I asked them to abandon this place, as my final wish. I keep myself short. I was once a researcher and still am. My experiments with Zro led to discover there potential for time manipulation. I call this process Chronomancy. Sadly it went wrong and afflicted me with a fatal illness. I am certain of my death and yet I can't leave my research behind. Take my greatest achievement: The $r_mem_aevum_houglass$ and use it for your own purposes.  Farewell dwellers of another era | `example/MEM-russian\mem_aevum_l_russian.yml` |
| `mem_aevum.11.g.response` | §HSome moments later he returns. No comments were made thereafter. Somehow, he looks way younger.  Creepy...§! | `example/MEM-russian\mem_aevum_l_russian.yml` |

| `mem_aevum_time_lord_catchphrase` | Time is relative, the Past not finished and Future is already unfolding. Only tomorrow knows what shall be. | `example/MEM-russian\mem_aevum_l_russian.yml` |
| `building_mem_eden_protocol_2_desc` | A facility with the purpose of seeding and maintaining organic life on habitable worlds within this solar system as well as terraforming regular terraforming candidates into habitable worlds. | `example/MEM-russian\mem_eden_protocol_l_russian.yml` |
| `mem_options.11.f` | Spawn one AI Leviathan. | `example/MEM-russian\mem_options_l_russian.yml` |

| `zone_mem_vazuran_support_ancestors_desc` | Dedicated smaller workshops in auxiliary role for the Relicforge. | `example/MEM-russian\mem_vazuran_menace_l_russian.yml` |
| `mem_eden_protocol.7.desc` | According to protocol, various groups of vat-grown sapients had been released on §Y[mem_eden_protocol_seeding_target.GetName]§!. Recent observations show them being well acclimated with their environment at this point.  Another successful seeding cycle. | `example/MEM-russian\mem_eden_protocol_l_russian.yml` |
| `MEM_VAZUR_HOSTILE_GREETING_2` | Talk fast. Our patience for lesser powers is microscopic. | `example/MEM-russian\mem_vazuran_menace_l_russian.yml` |
| `mem_czar_alexei.7.desc_1` | Our team arrived at the site of a crash that must have taken place decades ago. Foliage covered half of this rift sphere's wreckage. There were no signs of its former owners to be found. A quick inspection revealed apparently irreparable damage to the craft, which likely led to its abandonment. Its technology, however, appeared far more advanced than our own.   | `example/MEM-russian\mem_czar_alexei_l_russian.yml` |
| `mem_rubicon_mender_cap` | $mem_rubicon_mender$ | `example/MEM-russian\mem_ship_size_cap_l_russian.yml` |

| `mem_aevum.119.a` | Go ahead | `example/MEM-russian\mem_aevum_l_russian.yml` |

| `mem_ancestors_grudge.115.desc_robots` | During our reconstruction efforts, we have made a discovery: units from the ancient times before the fall, produced and stored for further processing. The mechanisms around them are worn and failing, yet their energy cells remain intact. We must act swiftly to stabilize the systems and bring them online. | `example/MEM-russian\mem_ancestors_grudge_l_russian.yml` |
| `mem_vazurans.6.d` | Ye be ugly as we remember, bug-face. | `example/MEM-russian\mem_vazuran_menace_l_russian.yml` |

| `mem_aevum_tooltip` | - Spawn the $mem_aevum_system$ System - Be the first Player to survey it. - Encounter $NAME_mem_aevum_time_lord$ and do his biddings .. or not - One way or another get access to his chronomancy cast menu - Use those abilities to shape your present | `example/MEM-russian\mem_aevum_l_russian.yml` |
| `mem_ancestors_grudge.110.desc` | During our reconstruction efforts, we have uncovered ancient defenses, battered and worn but not entirely lifeless. These ruins still hold enough of their original strength to be repaired and restored to their former might. Alternatively, we could dismantle them carefully and study their inner workings. | `example/MEM-russian\mem_ancestors_grudge_l_russian.yml` |
| `job_mem_ancestors_grudge_runesmith_catalytic_drone_plural` | $job_mem_ancestors_grudge_runesmith_catalytic_drone$ | `example/MEM-russian\mem_ancestors_grudge_l_russian.yml` |

| `mem_bird_thing` | Bird-Thing | `example/MEM-russian\mem_ancestors_grudge_l_russian.yml` |
| `mem_ancestors_grudge_official` | n §LOfficial§! | `example/MEM-russian\mem_ancestors_grudge_l_russian.yml` |
| `mem_mecha_desc_details` | This machine symbolizes the dreams of many of our engineers of present and past. Despite the common understanding of all the drawbacks that such a designs brings with it, the inhabitants of its world went for this concept wholeheartedly, as shown by its vast battlefields littered with these vehicles. | `example/MEM-russian\mem_specimens_l_russian.yml` |
| `mem_aevum.320.name` | My Final Message | `example/MEM-russian\mem_aevum_l_russian.yml` |
| `mem_aevum.4.a` | Alright, let us wait. | `example/MEM-russian\mem_aevum_l_russian.yml` |
| `mem_frontier_sector_2_leaders` | $specialist_mem_frontier_sector$ Centralization II | `example/MEM-russian\mem_ceaseless_l_russian.yml` |
| `mem_aevum_party_hard_desc` | Aevum's ever-changing desires have let to this party. The effect of Zro on him is not entirely understood, but it is certain that he has creative ways to get rid of it each month.  §YAevum's Boredom:§! [this.mem_aevum_boredom] | `example/MEM-russian\mem_aevum_l_russian.yml` |
| `zone_mem_vazuran_trains_secondary` | Rapid Logistic Nexus | `example/MEM-russian\mem_vazuran_menace_l_russian.yml` |
| `leader_trait_legendary_mem_ancestors_grudge_relicowner_official` | $leader_trait_legendary_mem_ancestors_grudge_relicowner$ | `example/MEM-russian\mem_ancestors_grudge_l_russian.yml` |
| `leader_trait_mem_metal_demon` | Living Metal Being | `example/MEM-russian\mem_metal_demon_l_russian.yml` |
| `mem_czar_alexei.11.desc.a` | Our team tried to break through the encirclement and get to safety but was met by fierce resistance. Weapon's fire from multiple directions mercilessly took them down one by one. $mem_czar_alexei.11.desc_2$ | `example/MEM-russian\mem_czar_alexei_l_russian.yml` |
| `mem_czar_alexei_sword_effect` | Ends all truces with other empires | `example/MEM-russian\mem_czar_alexei_l_russian.yml` |
| `mem_aevum_will_age_aevum` | Will age §Y$NAME_mem_aevum_time_lord$§! | `example/MEM-russian\mem_aevum_l_russian.yml` |
| `district_ancestors_grudge_relic_desc` | Enormous smeltery facilities supporting the ever lit furnaces of Runesmith workshops. | `example/MEM-russian\mem_ancestors_grudge_l_russian.yml` |
| `mem_ancestors_grudge_dranir_hall.1.name` | Visit $mem_ancestors_grudge_dranir$'s Hall | `example/MEM-russian\mem_ancestors_grudge_l_russian.yml` |
| `mem_aevum.15.name` | Captain's Log: Day 579 | `example/MEM-russian\mem_aevum_l_russian.yml` |
| `mem_crawler_statue_desc_details` | This apparently ancient stone statue bears remarkable similarities to our little guest. From its overall shape to the number of appendages and the structure of its mandibles the statue is an almost eerily close depiction. In fact some had thought it to be a real specimen repainted and glued to a base plate, but a scan confirmed it to be made of a single piece of solid rock. Whatever so-called cultist created it, they must have been a master of their art. Nevertheless, it is quite possible that other civilizations have had encounters with specimens of the crawler species in the past. Why it has been painted in such a negative light, however, is up for debate as we have never observed any aggressive tendencies from our actually quite docile guest so far. | `example/MEM-russian\mem_specimens_l_russian.yml` |
| `mem_VAZURAN_CARAPACE` | Xentronium Carapace | `example/MEM-russian\mem_vazuran_menace_l_russian.yml` |
| `mem_czar_alexei.12.desc.a` | Our team engaged the enemy in the open, but quickly found themselves overwhelmed by fire from multiple directions and was forced to take cover. Hunkered down behind debris the best they could do was to delay the inevitable and resist to the last.  A sudden explosion took out two of the attackers after which a close-quarters battle with an apparent third party ensued. With more and more attackers focusing their attention on the other force, our team took the initiative. Together with the new arrival, who as it turned out was only a single humanoid, the battle was won.  $mem_czar_alexei.12.desc_2$ | `example/MEM-russian\mem_czar_alexei_l_russian.yml` |
| `mem_aevum.11.g` | You should shave more | `example/MEM-russian\mem_aevum_l_russian.yml` |
| `Duras` | Duras | `example/MEM-russian\mem_ancestors_grudge_l_russian.yml` |
| `situation_mem_ancestors_grudge_forge_repairs_final_machines` | We will unlock our full potential | `example/MEM-russian\mem_ancestors_grudge_l_russian.yml` |
| `mem_czar_alexei.6.desc_1` | Our team arrived at the source of the broadcasted signal. Before them lay the wreckage of a crashlanded rift sphere. Judging by the rather recent damage to surrounding vegetation, it hasn't been here for very long. Despite there being clear signs of recent habitation, our scientists found no one around.  The exploration of its interior offered little clues as to the whereabouts of its crew. The ship itself is beyond repair, just like our own. To the surprise of our scientist, the sustained damage was very similar to our own ship.   | `example/MEM-russian\mem_czar_alexei_l_russian.yml` |
| `mod_job_mem_ancestors_grudge_relicsmith_per_pop` | §G+1§! $job_mem_ancestors_grudge_relicsmith$ Job per $VALUE$ Pops | `example/MEM-russian\mem_ancestors_grudge_l_russian.yml` |
| `mem_ancestors_grudge.26.desc` | After years of sweat, toil, and the strength of our hearts tested by many challenges, the work is done! The hold [target.GetName], once lost to ruin and ashes, now stands proud and whole once more.  [target.GetName] is reborn, its halls now echo golden to songs of yore re-sung. The sound of life and hammers is heard once more, its forges ablaze, and its strongholds a bulwark to defy time itself. The heart of [target.GetName] beats anew! | `example/MEM-russian\mem_ancestors_grudge_l_russian.yml` |
| `mem_aevum_speed_production_mod` | Production any% | `example/MEM-russian\mem_aevum_l_russian.yml` |
| `mem_aevum.210.b` | Improve our Research. | `example/MEM-russian\mem_aevum_l_russian.yml` |
| `mem_czar_alexei.3.b` | Head for the crash site. | `example/MEM-russian\mem_czar_alexei_l_russian.yml` |
| `mem_ancestors_grudge_rune_power` | $concept_mem_ancestors_grudge_rune_intensity$ | `example/MEM-russian\mem_ancestors_grudge_l_russian.yml` |
| `tr_mem_vazurans_1` | Dense Constructions | `example/MEM-russian\mem_vazuran_menace_l_russian.yml` |
| `mem_ceaseless_preftl_god` | Deus Ex Machina | `example/MEM-russian\mem_ceaseless_l_russian.yml` |
| `mem_ancestors_grudge.485.a_tt` | The predicted outcome will be lowered by one quality tier. | `example/MEM-russian\mem_ancestors_grudge_l_russian.yml` |
| `mem_aevum.110.name` | Timely Demise | `example/MEM-russian\mem_aevum_l_russian.yml` |
| `mem_ancestors_grudge.27.b.tt` | §YThe Dragon will be angry§! | `example/MEM-russian\mem_ancestors_grudge_l_russian.yml` |
| `REQUIRES_MEM_VAZURAN_DECISION_DISTRICT_SET` | §RRequires Ecumenopolis or equivalent Infrastructure!§! | `example/MEM-russian\mem_vazuran_menace_l_russian.yml` |
| `councilor_mem_subterranean_machine_female` | $councilor_mem_subterranean$ | `example/MEM-russian\mem_ancestors_grudge_l_russian.yml` |
| `situation_mem_ancestors_grudge_crafting_1` | Halt Ritual | `example/MEM-russian\mem_ancestors_grudge_l_russian.yml` |
| `mem_vazurans_raided_system` | Raided System | `example/MEM-russian\mem_vazuran_menace_l_russian.yml` |
| `mem_aevum.350.desc` | The guest kept observing our research. He did not interact with any of our staff except Dr. Tolith Eilleth. To be honest, it was not easy to get along with a different species. The starfish seemed rather friendly, but I could not decipher a single expression of the xeno, nor did his speech pattern and preference to speak in riddles help either. I heard the guest had brought some samples of regenerating tissue which could reverse their own cell decay. Dr. Thorne Eilleth was immenensly fascinated by these. | `example/MEM-russian\mem_aevum_l_russian.yml` |
| `mem_options.15.h` | Grand Admiral Zrawn | `example/MEM-russian\mem_options_l_russian.yml` |
| `mem_ancestors_grudge.28.name` | Dra-gone | `example/MEM-russian\mem_ancestors_grudge_l_russian.yml` |
| `mem_ancestors_grudge_dranir.2.a` | We have to go there! | `example/MEM-russian\mem_ancestors_grudge_l_russian.yml` |
| `leader_trait_legendary_mem_manifest_destiny_desc` | An ambition instilled in the very fabric of this man's self. The will to bend fate and achieve his vision of a future yet unseen by the galaxy. | `example/MEM-russian\mem_czar_alexei_l_russian.yml` |
| `mem_ancestors_grudge.490.b_tt` | The predicted maximum outcome will be lowered by one quality tier. | `example/MEM-russian\mem_ancestors_grudge_l_russian.yml` |
| `mem_ancestors_grudge.460.c` | Scrap all. | `example/MEM-russian\mem_ancestors_grudge_l_russian.yml` |
| `mem_ancestors_grudge.300.a` | Proceed. | `example/MEM-russian\mem_ancestors_grudge_l_russian.yml` |
| `concept_mem_ancestors_grudge_artifact_intensity_desc` | Determines the ['concept_mem_ancestors_grudge_rarity'] of ['concept_mem_ancestors_grudge_rune_relics'] | `example/MEM-russian\mem_ancestors_grudge_l_russian.yml` |
| `mem_ancestors_grudge.20.desc` | The work goes apace, with the stout hearts of the workers eager to clear away the rubble and filth of the ages.  More crucially, our clever minds studying the forge have struck upon grand discoveries! With what they have unearthed, we are now set to bring the forge back to full roaring life, as it was in the days of our forefathers. | `example/MEM-russian\mem_ancestors_grudge_l_russian.yml` |
| `mem_ancestors_grudge.45.i` | $mem_ancestors_grudge_quality_7$: Time for a Rematch | `example/MEM-russian\mem_ancestors_grudge_l_russian.yml` |
| `mem_aevum_mega_nuke` | Skip Particle Cannon | `example/MEM-russian\mem_aevum_l_russian.yml` |
| `district_mem_alloys_industry` | Plasma Forge | `example/MEM-russian\mem_vazuran_menace_l_russian.yml` |
| `mem_ancestors_grudge.305.desc` | The corridors stretch endlessly before us. Each time our teams round a corner, yet another corridor lies in wait, identical to the last. The air is thick with unease, and the silence broken only by the crunch of boots on ancient bones.  Skeletons litter the way. Countless dead whose stories remain untold and though we find what seems to be old supplies and rations, there seems little else of value. No chambers, no signs of life, only this maze of death and decay.  Still, the team stays motivated to march on. Whatever secrets these endless halls conceal, they shall be uncovered. Hopefully, they do not join the dead before finding the truth... | `example/MEM-russian\mem_ancestors_grudge_l_russian.yml` |
| `mem_ceaseless.145.title` | Independent One | `example/MEM-russian\mem_ceaseless_l_russian.yml` |
| `mem_ceaseless.115.desc` | Nearly a miss on initial scans, we found another probe. However, this one, designated as Probe 54T, seems changed. It appears to be attached to the surface of another wreck, even melted in some places. And it is not the only one. Curiously, the main wreckage seems to slowly absorb our probe as well as the other materiel while regenerating itself. | `example/MEM-russian\mem_ceaseless_l_russian.yml` |
| `mem_ancestors_grudge_dranir.60.desc` | Entire sections of the local defenses are reporting massive armies completely compromised of Ratmen slaves. These forces are being solely used to waste our ammunitions and drive us back. Early engagements report enemy loses in the millions. | `example/MEM-russian\mem_ancestors_grudge_l_russian.yml` |
| `mem_ancestors_grudge_is_hoarder_policy_science` | $mem_ancestors_grudge_is_hoarder_policy_science_name$ | `example/MEM-russian\mem_ancestors_grudge_l_russian.yml` |
| `mem_vazurans.1205.desc` | Reports all over the galaxy indicate an end of the vazuran raiding and an apparent return to normality. While none of the involved empires reported any relevant information a covert agents stationend within their territory report that the Vazuran Hegemony lost a considerable amount of manpower and material. Allied forces appear to have triangulated the source of the rifts and our troubles. We should now be able to jump into their home system and finish them off. | `example/MEM-russian\mem_vazuran_menace_l_russian.yml` |
| `mem_metal_demon.30.c` | How do we free you? | `example/MEM-russian\mem_metal_demon_l_russian.yml` |
| `mem_czar_alexei.16.hire.tooltip` | A §GSkill 5§! legendary commander joins your leader pool. | `example/MEM-russian\mem_czar_alexei_l_russian.yml` |
| `mem_vazurans.1310.desc` | Subjects, our forges have gone cold and we require additional laboureres ready to handle the workload. Since you all are occupying what is rightfully ours, we demand a tithe in laboureres for our mines. Don't waste our time and think about refusing. | `example/MEM-russian\mem_vazuran_menace_l_russian.yml` |
| `mem_aevum_building_temple` | Timeless Temple | `example/MEM-russian\mem_aevum_l_russian.yml` |
| `mem_aevum_beard_desc_short` | As $NAME_mem_aevum_time_lord$ parted ways with us and life, we shaved him one last time. Strangely enough, the beard does not seem to age | `example/MEM-russian\mem_aevum_l_russian.yml` |
| `d_mem_ancestors_grudge_dranir_asteroid_crater` | £blocker£ Asteroid Crater | `example/MEM-russian\mem_ancestors_grudge_l_russian.yml` |
| `mem_ancestors_grudge_dranir_ringworld_restored` | Orbital Mining Lasers | `example/MEM-russian\mem_ancestors_grudge_l_russian.yml` |
| `RULOC_eyu` |  | `example/MEM-russian\l_russian_ccloc.yml` |
| `mem_undead_thing` | Undead-Thing | `example/MEM-russian\mem_ancestors_grudge_l_russian.yml` |
| `district_mem_alloys_industry_desc` | A multitude of Xentronium based machinery geared towards a singular purpose: creating weapons | `example/MEM-russian\mem_vazuran_menace_l_russian.yml` |
| `mem_eden_protocol_mod_2_b` | $mem_eden_protocol_mod_1_a$ II | `example/MEM-russian\mem_eden_protocol_l_russian.yml` |
| `mem_aevum_alreadyfutureproof_tt` | £trigger_no£ We shouldn't send more people into the future. The first time was already stupid enough. | `example/MEM-russian\mem_aevum_l_russian.yml` |
| `personality_mem_vazuran_personality` | Ancient Threat | `example/MEM-russian\mem_vazuran_menace_l_russian.yml` |
| `building_mem_eden_protocol_1_desc` | A facility with the purpose of creating and maintaining suitable conditions for organic life on applicable worlds within this solar system. Through this, regular terraforming candidates will eventually be terraformed into habitable worlds. | `example/MEM-russian\mem_eden_protocol_l_russian.yml` |
| `mem_ancestors_grudge_commander` |  §LCommander§! | `example/MEM-russian\mem_ancestors_grudge_l_russian.yml` |
| `r_mem_aevum_houglass_desc` | Woven from time itself. Each grain a shimmering crystal. Cascading the narrow confines. Forgotten suns, lost civilizations and lucid visions...  The $r_mem_aevum_houglass$ has seen all and none. Past and future eternally bound to its circles. | `example/MEM-russian\mem_aevum_l_russian.yml` |
| `mem_ancestors_grudge_dragon_roost` | Dragon Roost | `example/MEM-russian\mem_ancestors_grudge_l_russian.yml` |
| `mem_rebel_yell_knight_cap` | $mem_rebel_yell_knight$ | `example/MEM-russian\mem_ship_size_cap_l_russian.yml` |
| `mem_XENTRONIUM_AURA_L` | Large $mem_VAZURAN_AURA$ | `example/MEM-russian\mem_vazuran_menace_l_russian.yml` |

| `mem_ancestors_grudge.490.title` | Strange Mood | `example/MEM-russian\mem_ancestors_grudge_l_russian.yml` |
| `mem_ceaseless_lost_probes_wormholes_charted` | Recovered Probe Data | `example/MEM-russian\mem_ceaseless_l_russian.yml` |
| `mem_aevum_party_hard` | The Never-Ending Party | `example/MEM-russian\mem_aevum_l_russian.yml` |
| `job_mem_ancestors_grudge_relicsmith_catalytic_drone_desc` | $job_mem_ancestors_grudge_relicsmith_drone_desc$ | `example/MEM-russian\mem_ancestors_grudge_l_russian.yml` |
| `mem_metal_demon.30.a.r` | Free me, and I offer you a new watershed moment for your young civilization. Living metal, a mineral that breathes and secrets buried beneath your feet. Your kind has stared at the stars, but I hold the keys to a treasure trove buried deep within this world. | `example/MEM-russian\mem_metal_demon_l_russian.yml` |
| `mem_ancestors_grudge.27.name` | From The Shadows | `example/MEM-russian\mem_ancestors_grudge_l_russian.yml` |
| `mem_ancestors_grudge_dranir.55.a` | Is this normal? | `example/MEM-russian\mem_ancestors_grudge_l_russian.yml` |
| `mem_aevum_beard_desc_details` | A truely magnificent beard wholly unheard of in any molluscoid species we have ever encountered, even in Aevum's own. It is save to say that its existence alone is an anomaly in and of itself. | `example/MEM-russian\mem_aevum_l_russian.yml` |
| `mem_ceaseless.140.a` | Analyze the probe's weakpoints. | `example/MEM-russian\mem_ceaseless_l_russian.yml` |
| `START_SCREEN_MEM_ANCESTORS_GRUDGE_HIVE` | For many centuries have we now ruled over [GetHomeWorldName]. It was the ruins of a war-torn world that we had found ourselves in. A shared consciousness had formed, giving our previous selves - little more than mindless beasts - purpose.  We explored this world and found ourselves strangers to it. Others had lived here before us, but only their ruins and strange artifacts remain. We have made it a habit to collect these shiny pieces and already amassed a sizeable hoard.  However, there is more that we long for. Our world's sky is dominated by three moons, but it is the closest, which draws us to it. We do not know why, but we feel we have to be there. It is callig for us. | `example/MEM-russian\mem_ancestors_grudge_l_russian.yml` |
| `mem_metal_demon.49.a` | A shackled demon? Now thats entertainment! | `example/MEM-russian\mem_metal_demon_l_russian.yml` |
| `mem_ancestors_grudge.449.a` | They are hard to please. | `example/MEM-russian\mem_ancestors_grudge_l_russian.yml` |
| `mem_czar_alexei.12.a` | Let us welcome the victorious! | `example/MEM-russian\mem_czar_alexei_l_russian.yml` |
| `mem_options.15.g` | Theophany God | `example/MEM-russian\mem_options_l_russian.yml` |
| `mem_vazurans_stance_tribute_tt` | §GNo Raids occur§! | `example/MEM-russian\mem_vazuran_menace_l_russian.yml` |
| `mem_ceaseless.105.desc` | Earlier scans indicated that Probe 7R was destroyed. Newer scans, however, reveal that no shot was fired and the probe was simply shredded to pieces.  What remains unclear, is what could possibly shred a giant vessel such as that probe in a matter of seconds... | `example/MEM-russian\mem_ceaseless_l_russian.yml` |
| `mem_aevum_button3_title` | §PJust-in-Time Research§! | `example/MEM-russian\mem_aevum_l_russian.yml` |
| `mem_czar_alexei.3.desc.a` | After a violent touchdown, our crew tensely awaits a finishing blow, so far nothing happened. According to our sensors, the attackers just passed over our crash site and left the immediate area. A superficial assessment of the sustained damage leaves little to no doubt that the needed repairs will be extensive if at all possible. With our tether gone, letting ourselves get reeled back, is out of the question.  We are close enough to the signal beacon to make our way on foot in the hope of finding help or usable materials there. Alternatively, we could take a detour to a nearby crash site which our scanners located earlier. Lastly, we could focus our efforts on repairing our sphere with what we have at hand. | `example/MEM-russian\mem_czar_alexei_l_russian.yml` |
| `mem_aevum.115.a` | Sure. | `example/MEM-russian\mem_aevum_l_russian.yml` |
| `mem_ancestors_grudge.485.b` | Let senior smiths offer their insight on his design flaws. | `example/MEM-russian\mem_ancestors_grudge_l_russian.yml` |

| `mem_czar_alexei_catchphrase` | Let our empire be a shining beacon, for the galaxy is dark and full of terrors. | `example/MEM-russian\mem_czar_alexei_l_russian.yml` |

| `STRANGE` | Strange | `example/MEM-russian\mem_aevum_l_russian.yml` |
| `d_mem_ancestors_grudge_ruined_stasis` | Frozen Ancestors | `example/MEM-russian\mem_ancestors_grudge_l_russian.yml` |
| `mem_czar_alexei.9.desc` | As planned out with the stranger, an ambush was set up to surprise the hunting party. Soon after the fighting began it became clear that they were aware of our plans. Our firing position was quickly outflanked and the battle turned into a one-sided slaughter.  Those who did not get killed during the fight were likely taken captive and are assumed missing in action. This marks the end of this ill-fated adventure. | `example/MEM-russian\mem_czar_alexei_l_russian.yml` |
| `mem_ancestors_grudge.205.desc` | Today, a new foe has dared to challenge us. They appeared at our gates, hammering down our defenses and had the audacity to lay claim to what is rightfully ours. Their boldness shall not go unanswered. They have sparked a new grudge. One that we shall see to its end. And see it settled in full, we will! | `example/MEM-russian\mem_ancestors_grudge_l_russian.yml` |
| `MEM_VAZUR_PEACE_ACCEPT` | We'll accept your offer. Consider it a rare stroke of mercy. | `example/MEM-russian\mem_vazuran_menace_l_russian.yml` |

| `mem_ancestors_grudge_dragon_roost_desc` | A dragon has chosen this world as its place to rest. Naturally this prevents any operation in the immediate area and diviancy is kept in check, lest the dragon might get hungry. | `example/MEM-russian\mem_ancestors_grudge_l_russian.yml` |

| `mem_ceaseless.100.desc` | Only a faint engine signature remains in this system. Probe 12L passed through here, surveyed some stellar bodies and left abruptly.  It is unclear to where, but it must have seemed important to it. Unfortunately for us, the trail has gone cold. | `example/MEM-russian\mem_ceaseless_l_russian.yml` |
| `mem_aevum.9.desc` | I commend your patience, yet, with utmost regret, I must inform you that fate, a relentless arbiter, has placed a trial on our destined summit.  Your fragile 20 years fall short to satisfy my required span. Let us meet in the middle, shall we?  I shall §Yreset your progress by 5 years§! and we converge in the aftermath. | `example/MEM-russian\mem_aevum_l_russian.yml` |
| `mem_vestigial_wormhole_science_ship_cap` | $mem_vestigial_wormhole_science_ship$ | `example/MEM-russian\mem_ship_size_cap_l_russian.yml` |

| `mem_aevum_buildtemple_desc` | The last of his trials. Hopefully. Let's build a kilostructure - the temple - for §Y$NAME_mem_aevum_time_lord$§!. | `example/MEM-russian\mem_aevum_l_russian.yml` |

| `mem_ancestors_grudge.50.desc` | Of legend and myth is the craft of the smith. A relic has been forged. A true masterpiece of our craft, and truly one of our finest creations to date. Let us now treasure what our hands have wrought, for such a work is not merely a tool, but a testament to the skill, strength, and spirit of our ancestors. May it serve as a symbol of what we can achieve, when runes, durasteel, and resolve unite!  Quality: [owner.MemAncestorsGrudgeQualityFinal] Purity: [owner.MemAncestorsGrudgePurityFinal] | `example/MEM-russian\mem_ancestors_grudge_l_russian.yml` |

| `mem_aevum_sit_wait_20years` | Delayed Gratification  | `example/MEM-russian\mem_aevum_l_russian.yml` |
| `mem_ancestors_grudge_dranir_warprock_desc_details` | These stones were used to power varies machines of war and enables the Ratmen to swiftly move forces. | `example/MEM-russian\mem_ancestors_grudge_l_russian.yml` |
| `mem_ancestors_grudge_kraken` | Charybdis | `example/MEM-russian\mem_ancestors_grudge_l_russian.yml` |

| `building_mem_ancestors_grudge_hoarder_depot_desc` | A giant vault. Some would even say the greatest vault ever built. Its true purpose is to store all the precious relics collected by its owner. | `example/MEM-russian\mem_ancestors_grudge_l_russian.yml` |
| `mem_ancestors_grudge.41.a` | Save the Shoulder Guards! | `example/MEM-russian\mem_ancestors_grudge_l_russian.yml` |

| `mem_aevum_plushie_site_desc` | Inital scans revealed faint traces of energy signatures. Further investigation revealed abandoned orbital infrastructure. The desolated complex lies in wait for us to reveal its former purpose. | `example/MEM-russian\mem_aevum_l_russian.yml` |
| `mem_czar_alexei.16.desc_01` | That I am not sure about yet.  I could join you and share my expertise in various fields, if you are willing to offer me a place. Afterall I am in your debt.
